product: Confluent REST Proxy (Confluent Platform, current docs) — v2 produce API

version_choice.why_not_v3: the current docs also describe a v3 produce endpoint (POST /v3/clusters/{cluster_id}/topics/{topic_name}/records, media type application/json) that takes one record per request/stream item; the batch body with a per-record offsets array, embedded-format media types and the null-key schema-exclusion rule below are the v2 contract, which is what this producer needs

request_body.records: array of {key?, value?, partition?}; this client omits the key/value/partition members entirely when unset (null key, tombstone value, proxy-chosen partition)
request_body.key_schema/value_schema: full schema string for schema-embedded formats (avro, jsonschema, protobuf); never sent for json/binary
request_body.null_rule: when every record key is null the request must exclude key_schema and key_schema_id; same for values and value_schema/value_schema_id
request_body.empty_batch: client-side rule: an empty records slice is rejected locally and never reaches the wire

response_body.key_schema_id: int or null — registered schema id echoed back
response_body.offsets: array, same order as the request records, of {partition: int|null, offset: long|null, error_code: long|null, error: string|null}

partial_failure.http_status: 200 even when individual records fail — per-record error_code must be inspected
partial_failure.error_code_2: retriable Kafka exception; the message might be sent successfully if retried
partial_failure.retry_rule: retries re-send ONLY records whose error_code was 2, merge outcomes back into the original record positions, and never re-send a record that already succeeded; attempts are bounded and separated by an injected backoff callback; context cancellation between attempts returns the merged partial result plus the context error

request_level_errors.422/42202: schema-embedded records without value_schema or value_schema_id

auth_and_redirects.scheme: Basic auth on every request to the configured origin
auth_and_redirects.redirects: never followed: any 3xx produce response surfaces as an APIError with that HTTP status and no request is made to the redirect target
